evals.notes: with_skill achieved 73% reduction (50 lines) vs without_skill 57% (79 lines). Skill produced more aggressive but correct optimization.
with_skill kept ## Dependencies header as pointer instead of fully removing. Baseline was more aggressive in removal.
Both correctly identified file as already optimal. No changes made.

analyst_observations: with_skill excels at the bloated case: 73% reduction vs 57% baseline, while preserving all high-value content. The structured classification framework drives more aggressive removal.

with_skill's Pointer migration strategy caused A10 failure: it converts Dependencies to a See-Cargo.toml pointer instead of removing the section entirely. This is arguably correct behavior (the pointer is useful) but fails the strict assertion.
Both configurations handle the minimal case identically — this assertion set is non-discriminating for already-optimized files.

with_skill costs ~20% more tokens and ~44% more time due to the 5-step structured process (SCAN/CLASSIFY/PLAN/REWRITE/VERIFY). The overhead is meaningful but the quality improvement on bloated files justifies it.
Baseline over-removed content in mixed case: dropped Monitoring URLs (Grafana dashboard) which are actionable references. with_skill correctly preserved these.
